name: Mount Kagu
shortDescription: Mount Kagu is a sacred mountain in Japanese mythology, believed to be a source of divine power and a significant location in creation myths.
description: Mount Kagu (香具山, Kagu-yama), also known as Ama-no-Kagu-yama (天香具山, Heavenly Mount Kagu), is a sacred mountain in Japanese mythology and Shinto. It is considered one of the most important mountains in Japanese folklore, often depicted as a source of divine energy and a pivotal location in the creation narratives. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, Mount Kagu played a crucial role in the story of Amaterasu's seclusion in the Ama-no-Iwato (heavenly rock cave). When Amaterasu hid herself, plunging the world into darkness, the kami (gods) gathered on the bank of the Ama-no-Yasu-gawa River to strategize. It was from Mount Kagu that the sakaki tree, adorned with jewels (yata-no-kagami) and offerings, was uprooted and used to lure Amaterasu out of the cave. The mountain is therefore strongly associated with the restoration of light and order to the world. Mount Kagu is not merely a physical landmark but a symbolic representation of the divine realm and the connection between the earthly and heavenly planes. Its significance extends to purification rituals (harae) and the general worship of kami. The mountain is also linked to Takamagahara, the High Celestial Plain, suggesting a direct connection to the abode of the gods. In some accounts, the mountain itself is considered to have descended from the heavens, further emphasizing its divine origins.

location.cosmological: Believed to be a sacred mountain with a direct connection to Takamagahara, the High Celestial Plain.

places.name: Ama-no-Iwato
